meta.title: System Vision & Principles
meta.excerpt: Enterprise-grade architectural patterns derived from proven C#/.NET practices, adapted for the modern TypeScript/Next.js ecosystem.

seo.metaDescription: Learn the core architectural vision, enterprise-grade design principles, and foundational values that guide our Next.js documentation platform.
seo.keywords: architecture, system design, principles, vision, next.js, typescript, enterprise patterns

blocks.content: Our system is built on enterprise-grade architectural patterns derived from proven C#/.NET best practices including TDD, SOLID principles, MVVM, and Domain-Driven Design. These patterns have been carefully adapted for the modern TypeScript/Next.js ecosystem to ensure we maintain the same rigor and clarity in JavaScript that enterprise systems have enjoyed in the .NET world.

This application implements enterprise-grade architectural patterns that prioritize clarity, maintainability, and scalability. Every decision we make fits into a cohesive vision where data flows cleanly through layers, each with a single responsibility. We believe in building systems that are easy to understand, test, and evolve.
Five core principles guide all architectural decisions and shape how we structure code, data access, and components:

items.title: Separation of Concerns
items.description: Data layer → Domain layer → Presentation layer. Each tier has a single responsibility and communicates through well-defined contracts.

items.title: Backend Agnostic
items.description: Repository abstraction allows easy backend swap (Strapi → oRPC → GraphQL). Infrastructure changes should not ripple through application code.

items.title: Type Safety
items.description: Contracts at every boundary, validated at both build time and runtime. The type system is our first line of defense against bugs.

items.title: Graceful Degradation
items.description: Errors are isolated to the smallest blast radius. A single data fetch failure should not crash the entire page.

items.title: Component Reusability
items.description: Features layer enables components to work across different contexts. We extract patterns and standardize them rather than duplicating code.

blocks.content: Our technology choices are deliberate and aligned with our architectural vision. Each tool was selected because it enables one or more of our core principles.

items.title: Framework: Next.js 16+
items.description: App Router with React Server Components for clean separation between server-only data access and client-side interactivity

items.title: Language: TypeScript 5+
items.description: Strict mode enabled everywhere, enforcing 100% type coverage and catching entire categories of bugs at compile time

items.title: Validation: Zod
items.description: Runtime schema validation that provides both data validation and TypeScript type inference from a single schema definition

items.title: Styling: Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui
items.description: Utility-first CSS with a component library of accessible, composable UI primitives

items.title: CMS (Future): Strapi 5+
items.description: Currently mocked with JSON, Strapi will provide headless CMS capabilities with full type integration

items.title: Data Fetching (Future): oRPC
items.description: End-to-end type safety with chaining, enabling full-stack type inference from database to UI

blocks.title: Enterprise-Grade Standards
blocks.content: This architecture is designed to scale from a single-person team to large organizations. It enforces the discipline and clarity that enterprise systems require, while remaining agile and developer-friendly.
